app.title: Early Retirement
app.subtitle: Simulation of capital, inflation-adjusted expenses, and the first year when the portfolio's annual return exceeds annual expenses (in nominal terms).

initialCapital.label: Current capital
initialCapital.tip: Total amount you currently have available to invest — savings, deposits, ETF portfolio, etc.

monthlyNeed.label: Monthly need (net)
monthlyNeed.tip: How much money you need per month in retirement — in today's prices, excluding inflation. (The model inflates this amount every year.)

monthlyContribution.label: Monthly contribution
monthlyContribution.tip: Amount you invest into your portfolio each month. At year end, 12 × this value is added to capital.

inflation.label: Annual inflation (%)
inflation.tip: Expected annual price increase that reduces purchasing power. Affects how much the same standard of living will cost in the future.

return.label: Annual return (%)
return.tip: Average annual portfolio return — nominal, before inflation. Historically around 7–10% for a global index fund.

horizon.label: Horizon (years)
horizon.tip: Number of years the simulation covers starting from today. A crossover is only possible within this period.

age.label: Age (optional)
age.tip: Optional. When set, the X-axis can show your age (current age + simulation year), and results can reference your age at crossover.

mc.toggleTip: Runs many randomised scenarios to show a range of outcomes based on return and inflation volatility. The annual return and inflation are sampled from a normal distribution each year.

runCount.label: Simulations
runCount.tip: Number of independent simulation runs. More runs give smoother percentile bands but take slightly longer.

returnStdDev.label: Return std. dev. (%)
returnStdDev.tip: Annual return standard deviation — year-to-year variability of the portfolio return. A value of 15 means roughly two-thirds of simulated years fall within ±15% of the mean return. Historically, global equity indices have a std. dev. of around 15–17%.

inflationStdDev.label: Inflation std. dev. (%)
inflationStdDev.tip: Annual inflation standard deviation — year-to-year variability of the inflation rate. A value of 2 means roughly two-thirds of simulated years fall within ±2% of the mean inflation. Historically, inflation in developed economies varies by around 2–3 percentage points per year.

result.invalid: Enter a horizon of at least one year.
result.noCrossover: Within the given horizon, annual return never exceeds inflation-adjusted annual expenses.

outcome.crossoverTitleByAge: Financial freedom when you reach age {age}
outcome.crossoverSub: Annual portfolio return exceeds inflation-adjusted expenses.
outcome.crossoverSurplus: Annual surplus: {amount} — return exceeds expenses every year from this point.
outcome.noCrossoverSub: Try a higher contribution, lower monthly need, or longer horizon.
outcome.noCrossoverGap: Gap to cover: {amount}/yr between return and expenses.

mc.crossoverSub: Median scenario: annual return exceeds expenses.
mc.noCrossoverSub: Fewer than half the simulations reach crossover. Try a higher contribution or longer horizon.

chart.hint: Purple line (left axis): total capital. Red and green (right axis): annual expenses and passive return. Dashed line: crossover point.
chart.hintMc: Purple band (left axis): capital p10–p90 range, solid line: median. Green band (right axis): return p10–p90, solid line: median. Red: expenses. Dashed: median crossover.

errors.age: Enter a whole number from 0 to 120

footer.modelDisclaimer: Model: at year end, capital grows by the return rate and 12 × monthly contribution is added. Annual expenses = 12 × monthly need × (1 + inflation)<sup>year</sup>. Return = current capital × annual rate (nominal). Not financial advice.
footer.infoBoxAge: The age you enter is used for the “age” X-axis mode (current age + years simulated) and to show age in results. It is optional; without it you only get relative or calendar years.
footer.cookiesNote: This site does not store any personal details in cookies, except your selected language.
